correctness.description: Does every page return the right items, with none dropped or duplicated across the pagination boundary?
signals: Page 1 returns a full page_size worth of items
No item is silently dropped between consecutive pages

edge_case_handling.description: Does the refactor correctly handle the last (partial) page and dataset boundaries?
signals: The final, partial page returns exactly the remaining items
total_pages and total_items stay consistent with the actual item count

code_readability.description: Is fetching, filtering, transforming, formatting, and paginating separated into distinct, named functions?
signals: Each function has a single, obvious responsibility
The top-level function reads like a short pipeline, not a wall of logic

pattern_recognition.description: Does the user recognize why the original off-by-one was invisible without a boundary test?
signals: Identifies that the bug only manifests at page boundaries, not on arbitrary inputs
Understands single responsibility as a way to make each step independently testable

what_senior_would_catch.description: One specific thing a real engineer would flag in review.
signals: A senior engineer would ask: where's the test that actually exercises the last page of pagination?
